title: kdb+ Cheat Sheet
description: Essential kdb+ commands and patterns for database management.

commands.description: Load a q script into the kdb+ environment.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to load and execute a q script file.

commands.description: Select rows from a table where the symbol is AAPL.
commands.scenario: Use to filter data in a table based on a condition.

commands.description: Insert a new row into the trades table.
commands.scenario: Use to add new data to a table.

commands.description: Delete rows from a table where the symbol is GOOG.
commands.scenario: Use to remove specific data from a table.

commands.command: q)update price:price*1.1 from trades where sym=`AAPL
commands.description: Update prices in the trades table for AAPL by 10%.
commands.scenario: Use to modify existing data in a table.

commands.command: q)select sum size by sym from trades
commands.description: Aggregate data by summing sizes grouped by symbol.
commands.scenario: Use to perform aggregation operations on table data.

commands.description: Time the execution of a query.
commands.scenario: Use to measure the performance of a query.
Use to organize code into different namespaces.

commands.description: Display the schema of the trades table.
commands.scenario: Use to inspect the structure of a table.

commands.description: Get metadata information of the trades table.
commands.scenario: Use to view metadata such as column types of a table.

commands.description: Save a table to a CSV file.
commands.scenario: Use to export table data to a CSV file for external use.

commands.description: Set the port for the kdb+ server.
commands.scenario: Use to change the port number for server connections.
